meaning: n.楠氫贡,鍔ㄨ崱,(娑蹭綋鎴栨皵浣撶殑)绱婁贡
examples: The 1960s and early 1970s were a time of change and turbulence.
The plane encountered severe turbulence and winds of nearly two-hundred miles an hour.
On the other hand, air turbulence increases because of the effect of skyscrapers on airflow.
By that time, the captain had turned on the seat belt sign for some expected turbulence.
In fact, take all such daily explanations of market turbulence with a grain of salt.
